title: ClientProfile
description: Structured client profile produced by the onboarding agent after completing all 10 discovery stages. This file defines what profile.json must contain and what each field means.

client_id.description: Lowercase hyphenated client identifier. Must match the folder name under memory/client_profiles/. Example: acme-corp

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the onboarding session was first started

completed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the profile was compiled (Stage 10 completed)

completed_stages.description: List of stage numbers that have been completed and saved. Used by the startup routine to determine where to resume.

readiness_score.description: Client readiness signal calculated at Stage 10. Based on completeness and depth of all collected data.

points.description: Numeric score. Starts at 100. Deductions: -5 per vague optional field, -15 per vague required field, -20 per skipped required field, -10 per refused field.

flags.description: All flagged fields that contributed to score deductions

field.description: The field path that was flagged, e.g. voice.tone_descriptors

note.description: Brief explanation of why it was flagged

industry.description: The industry or sector they operate in

description.description: What the business does in plain English. Minimum 2 sentences. Must be specific enough for a stranger to understand.

differentiator.description: What makes them different from competitors. Must be specific — not 'we care more' or 'we deliver results'.

stage.description: Where the business is in its lifecycle

years_operating.description: Approximate number of years in business

location.description: Primary location or market served (city, country, or 'global')

team_size.description: Approximate number of people. Can be a range or descriptor: '1 (solo)', '2-5', '10-50', etc.

primary_offer.description: The main product, service, or program the client sells

secondary_offers.description: Other products or services offered, if any

price_range.description: Approximate price. Can be a range or descriptor: '$97', '$1,000-$5,000', 'custom enterprise pricing', etc.

price_tier.description: Broad pricing category. low_ticket = under ~$500. mid_ticket = $500-$5k. high_ticket = $5k+.

delivery_model.description: How the offer is delivered to the client

outcomes.description: Specific, concrete results clients get from the offer. Must be measurable or tangible — not 'they grow their business'.

objections.description: Real objections heard from prospects who are close to buying but haven't yet

transformation.description: The before/after story. What does the client's situation look like before vs. after working with this business?

primary_segment.description: The primary target audience described specifically. Not just 'entrepreneurs' — enough detail to picture a real person.

secondary_segments.description: Other audience segments served, if any

income_range.description: Household or business revenue range, if relevant

job_titles.description: For B2B: job titles of the people who buy or influence the decision

company_sizes.description: For B2B: company size ranges by headcount or revenue. e.g. '10-50 employees', '$1M-$10M revenue'

psychographics.description: Mindset, beliefs, and motivations of the audience

values.description: What the audience cares about deeply — beyond their immediate problem

aspirations.description: What they want to achieve, become, or be known for

frustrations.description: Day-to-day frustrations — not just the big problem, but the texture of what bothers them

self_identity.description: How they see themselves — the identity label they'd use: 'I'm a founder', 'I'm a creator', etc.

buying_triggers.description: What causes them to finally decide to buy — the tipping point events or emotions

pain_points.description: Specific, detailed pain points. Must be detailed enough to write content to them — not just 'they need more leads'.

existing_beliefs.description: What the audience already believes before encountering this brand — including misconceptions that hold them back

online_hangouts.description: Where the audience spends time online — platforms, communities, newsletters, podcasts

tone_descriptors.description: 3-5 adjectives describing the brand voice. Generic words like 'professional' must be accompanied by examples or clarification.

never_sound_like.description: What the brand should never sound like. At least one specific example — not just 'don't be salesy'.

formality.description: Where the brand sits on the formality spectrum

description.description: What the reference is (brand name, publication, creator, type of content)

what_they_like.description: Specifically what they like about it — tone, topics, format, attitude, etc.

reference_content.description: Content, brands, or creators the client loves — and specifically what they like about them

anti_reference_content.description: Content styles, brands, or approaches the client hates or wants to avoid

personality_in_one_sentence.description: If the brand were a person, describe them in one sentence. e.g. 'A sharp-witted strategist who tells it straight and has no patience for fluff.'

primary_goal.description: The single most important business outcome content should drive

primary_kpi.description: The measurable metric that defines success. Must be specific: 'booked discovery calls per month', not 'more engagement'.

target_kpi_value.description: The actual number or threshold being aimed for, if known. e.g. '10 booked calls/month', '500 new subscribers in 90 days'

timeline.description: When results are expected or when key dates occur. e.g. '3-6 months', 'product launch in September'

content_budget_signal.description: Rough signal of how much is being invested in content. bootstrap = minimal; invested = significant budget allocated.

what.description: What was tried — platform, format, approach

why_failed.description: Why it didn't work — from the client's perspective

previous_attempts.description: Previous content efforts that didn't achieve the desired result

primary_platform.description: The single most important platform for content distribution

secondary_platforms.description: Other platforms where content will be distributed

formats.description: Content formats in use. e.g. 'short-form text posts', 'long-form articles', 'carousels', 'email newsletter', 'short-form video'

posting_frequency.description: Posting frequency per platform. Keys are platform names (e.g. 'linkedin', 'newsletter', 'instagram').

posts_per_week.description: Number of posts per week on this platform

posts_per_month.description: Number of posts per month on this platform (use if weekly cadence doesn't apply)

notes.description: Any nuance about the cadence for this platform

platform.description: Email platform being used. e.g. Beehiiv, Substack, ConvertKit, Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign

send_day.description: Preferred or current send day. e.g. 'Tuesday mornings'

profile_type.description: Whether content goes on personal profile, company page, or both

current_followers.description: Approximate current follower or connection count

has_production_capacity.description: Whether the client can film and produce video themselves or has someone who can

raw_footage_available.description: Whether existing raw footage is available for repurposing

editing_handled_by.description: Who handles video editing. e.g. 'client', 'in-house editor', 'outsourced', 'agency'

publishing_platform.description: Where the podcast is hosted and distributed. e.g. Spotify, Apple Podcasts, Buzzsprout

current_downloads.description: Approximate downloads per episode if known

what_they_do_well.description: What this competitor does well — from the client's perspective

what_they_do_poorly.description: Where this competitor falls short — the gap the client can exploit

why_admired.description: Specifically what is admired — tone, format, topics, consistency, etc.

content_inspirations.description: Brands or creators the client admires — inside or outside their industry

differentiators.description: What sets this client apart from their competition — specific and ownable claims

do_not_copy.description: Specific things competitors do that the client refuses to do or replicate

off_limits_topics.description: Topics, statements, or angles that must never appear in content

requirement.description: The specific compliance requirement or restriction

industry_regulation.description: The regulation or body it comes from. e.g. HIPAA, FCA, SEC, FTC, GDPR

sensitive_areas.description: Topics or angles that require care but aren't fully off-limits

past_problems.description: Content-related issues that have caused problems in the past

explicitly_confirmed_no_constraints.description: Set to true if the client explicitly confirmed they have no constraints. Distinguishes 'no constraints' from a skipped stage.

primary_reviewer.description: The person who reviews content first. Can be the same as final_approver.

additional_reviewers.description: Other people whose input is required before approval

final_approver.description: The person whose sign-off means content can be published

revision_rounds.description: Typical number of revision rounds expected before approval

turnaround_days.description: How many business days the client typically needs to review and provide feedback

feedback_format.description: How the client prefers to give feedback

preferred_delivery_format.description: How the client wants content delivered for review. e.g. 'Google Doc', 'Notion page', 'email', 'shared drive'
